description: Geographic spine for the platform. Defines the police administrative hierarchy and the keys used to join it to Census 2011 and LGD (Local Government Directory) geographies for socio-economic correlation.

pilot_state.name: Karnataka
pilot_state.note: Pilot state for Phase 1. Karnataka uses Police Commissionerates for major cities (Bengaluru City Police is a Commissionerate headed by a Commissioner) plus rural Police Districts grouped under Ranges. Commissionerate maps to the RANGE/DISTRICT tiers below.

levels.name: Police Range / Commissionerate / Zone
levels.note: Police-only administrative tier. In Karnataka a Range groups rural districts under an IGP; a city Commissionerate is the urban equivalent. No census equivalent.

levels.name: Police District
levels.note: PRIMARY analytical grain. Police districts can differ from revenue/census districts; see crosswalk.

levels.name: Police Station
levels.note: Operational grain for district/station drilldowns. Jurisdiction polygon where available.

levels.name: Beat
levels.note: Finest operational unit; often unavailable in open data.

crosswalk.purpose: Police districts != revenue/census districts in several states. A crosswalk table maps each police DISTRICT to one or more census/LGD district codes (and weights for splitting socio-economic figures).
crosswalk.note: allocation_weight sums to 1.0 across the census districts a police district overlaps; used to apportion district-level socio-economic indicators.

geometry.note: Where station jurisdiction polygons are unavailable, use district polygon + station point (geocoded address) and assign incidents to district centroid.

id_convention.rule: geo_unit_id is globally unique and stable; parent_id references the id of the level above.
